message: I've been diving into home fire planning lately, and honestly? It's one of those things that feels way less overwhelming once you actually start thinking it through. I found this solid resource that walks you through the practical stuff—escape routes, meeting spots, what to do if you can't get out—without making it scary. The nice part is realizing that a little planning actually gives you *peace* of mind instead of taking it away. What's one room in your home where you'd want to make sure everyone knows a safe exit?
